name: Perun Extended
description: Perun, the supreme god of the Slavic pantheon, reigns as the embodiment of thunder, lightning, storms, war, and justice. He is not merely a destroyer, but a cosmic force of order, battling the chaotic forces embodied by Veles, the god of the underworld and trickery. Perun wields a mighty hammer, stone axes, and arrows of lightning, which he hurls down from the heavens to punish the wicked and maintain the balance of the world. His sacred tree is the oak, and his colors are red and black. He is revered as the protector of warriors, princes, and oaths, ensuring loyalty and integrity. Perun's influence extends beyond the battlefield; he is also invoked for fertility and rain, essential for the prosperity of the land. His festivals were often celebrated during the harvest season, with offerings of bulls, oxen, and other animals. Perun's role is not simply one of brute force, but also of a wise and just ruler, setting the standards for morality and social order. He is a symbol of strength, courage, and unwavering commitment to upholding the cosmic law. The clash between Perun and Veles is a recurring theme in Slavic mythology, representing the eternal struggle between good and evil, order and chaos. Perun's legacy continues to resonate in Slavic folklore and traditions, reminding people of the importance of courage, justice, and respect for the natural world. He is often depicted as a strong, bearded man, riding a chariot pulled by goats or horses, a fearsome warrior whose wrath is both terrifying and necessary.
subtitle: Thunderer, Warrior, God of Justice

keyMyths.title: Perun vs. Veles
keyMyths.description: Perun battles Veles, the god of the underworld, to retrieve stolen cattle and maintain cosmic order. This conflict represents the eternal struggle between good and evil.

keyMyths.title: The Sacred Oak
keyMyths.description: The oak tree is sacred to Perun, symbolizing his strength and connection to the earth. Offerings were often made at the base of oak trees in his honor.

keyMyths.title: Perun's Hammer
keyMyths.description: Perun's hammer is a symbol of his power, used to create thunder and lightning. It represents his ability to enforce justice and maintain balance.

sources.author: Helmold of Bosau
sources.description: A historical account mentioning the worship of Slavic deities, including Perun.
